title: Bhairavī (Proper) Sādhana
subcategory: Mahāvidyā (fifth) — the teacher-aspect of the Goddess
century: Medieval (c. 9th–14th c.); modern digests c. 19th–20th c.
description: The fifth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās in the canonical Śākta list, Bhairavī (proper) is the Goddess as the teacher (*guru-rūpiṇī*) — distinct from Tripura-Bhairavī (the sixth Mahāvidyā, the fierce form of Tripurasundarī, covered in *tripura-bhairavi-sadhana.md*). Bhairavī proper is the Goddess of *tapas* (austerity) in her universal cosmic form, holding a *mālā* (rosary) and a *pustaka* (book), seated
